text: Great whites grow up to 20 feet long, and can pack over 5,000 pounds of muscle.
Yet, despite their size, they often go unnoticed.
A type of camouflage, known as countershading gives these sharks the predatory edge.
They're dark on top, but pale underneath.
So underwater, they blend into the background, whether seen from above or below.
